claim: The rooted IQ-TREE topology stayed unchanged across every declared alignment and trimming variant.
evidence: 6 rooted pairwise preprocessing comparisons returned RF distance 0 and no rooting-only disagreements.
caution: This stability statement is limited to the compact nine-taxon rabies panel and the four declared preprocessing settings.

claim: The selected substitution model remained constant across the declared preprocessing matrix.
caution: Model-selection stability here reflects one short rabies nucleoprotein panel rather than a general claim about all pathogen alignments.

claim: After explicit outgroup rooting, FastTree and IQ-TREE preserved the same rooted topology in every declared preprocessing variant.
evidence: rooted engine comparisons over outgroup bat_chile_rv108 returned RF distance 0 in every variant.
caution: Rooted agreement does not imply that every internal unrooted split or support value is interchangeable across engines.

claim: Before rooting, the FastTree versus IQ-TREE comparison changed several internal clade conclusions on this rabies panel.
evidence: serious unrooted engine conflicts ranged up to 8 per variant, while stable shared clades per variant ranged from 2 to 2.
caution: The engine-sensitive clades are a warning against over-reading fine internal structure from one compact panel, especially when approximate and likelihood engines disagree before rooting.
